semanticCore: Open space, cavity, wide opening, gap, or spacious chamber
quranicRelevance: In an open space within the cave (Fajwah - 'fee fajwatin minhu').

keyDerivations.meaning: Open space, wide chamber, gap, inner cavity, or spacious yard

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَتَرَى الشَّمْسَ إِذَا طَلَعَتْ تَزَاوَرُ عَنْ كَهْفِهِمْ ... وَهُمْ فِي فَجْوَةٍ مِنْهُ (18:17): And you would see the sun, when it rose, inclining away from their cave... while they were in an open space/spacious chamber within it.
summaryEn: The root indicates a spacious interior opening or gap (Fajw), describing the cave's natural architecture which allowed the Sleepers to enjoy ventilation without direct sunlight.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى المتسع الساحي من الأرض والفرجة والفتحة بين شيئين (فجو)، واصفاً استقرار أصحاب الكهف في بهو داخلي واسع من الكهف يضمن لهم الهواء والظل.
rawText: أَفْجَى an epithet, of which the fem. is فَجْوَآءُ. (K, TA.) The latter, applied to a bow, Having its string distant from the part called its كَبِد; (S, K, TA;) as also 🡻فَجُوْءٌ; and so فَجَّآءُ [mentioned in art. فج]. (Er-Rághib, TA.) === And the former, (K, TA,) applied to a man, (TA,) Wide between the thighs, or between the knees, or between the shanks: or, applied to a camel, wide between the hocks: (K, TA:) or, accord. to Az, it signifies having the thighs very wide apart. (TA.) [Freytag adds “ Ventrosus, ” applied to a camel, as from the K, in which I do not find it.] [Accord. to the TA, some of the words of this art. have ى for the final radical; but for this distinction there is no reason.] افجى 4 He expended amply, or largely, upon his family, or household. (Az, K.) ___ And He found his friend to be guilty of a vice, or a disgraceful, or shameful, action. (Az, TA.) انفجى 7 It (a door) opened. (K.) === See also 1. تفاجى 6 It (a thing) had [an opening, or intermediate wide space, such as is termed] a فَجْوَة. (S, TA.) [Comp. تَفَاجَّ, in art. فج.] فجّى 2 , (TA,) inf. n. تَفْجِيَةٌ, (K, TA,) He removed; put away, or at a distance; (K, * TA;) and pushed, thrust, or drove, away; persons from others; (TA;) syn. of the inf. n. كَشْفٌ; and تَنْحِيَةٌ; (K, TA;) and دَفْعٌ. (TA.) فَجًا inf. n. of فَجِيَتْ [q. v.] said of a bow: (S, TA:) === and of فَجِىَ [q. v.] said of a man, (K, * TA,) or of a camel: (S, * K, TA:) === and of فَجِيَتْ [q. v.] said of a she-camel. (K, TA.) فَجَا بَابَهُ 1 , (K,) [aor. يَفْجُو,] inf. n. فَجْوٌ, (TA,) He opened his door. (K.) === And فَجَا القَوْسَ, (S, K,) inf. n. as above, (S,) He raised the string of the bow [or made it distant] from the part called its كَبِد: (S, K:) and so فَجَّهَا. (S and K in art. فج.) === And فَجَا رِجْلَيْهِ, or مَا بَيْنَ رِجْلَيْهِ, He parted his legs wide, or straddled; or did so to make water: and so فَجَّ. (TA in art. فج.) ___ فَجِيَتْ, (S, K, TA, [in the CK, and in one of my copies of the S, erroneously, فَجِئَت,]) aor. تَفْجَى, inf. n. فَجًا, (S, TA,) The bow had its string raised [or distant] from the part called its كَبِد; (S, K;) and so 🡻اِنْفَجَت. (ISd, TA.) === And فَجِىَ, [in the CK, erroneously, فَجِئَ,] aor. as above, (K, TA,) and so the inf. n., i. e. فَجًا, (K, * TA,) He (a man, TA) was wide between the thighs, or between the knees, or between the shanks. (K, TA.) [And it is implied in the S * and K that it is also said of a camel, meaning He was wide between the hocks.] === And فَجِيَتْ said of a she-camel, inf. n. فَجًا, She was, or became, large in the belly: (K, TA:) mentioned by ISd, but with an expression of uncertainty as to its correctness. (TA.) فَجُوْءٌ : see the following paragraph. فَجْوَآءُ [as a subst.]: see the next preceding paragraph. [It is originally the fem. of the epithet أَفْجَى, q. v.] فَجْوَةٌ An opening, or intervening space, (S, M, Mgh, Msb, K,) in a place, (M, TA,) and an intermediate wide space, (S, Mgh,) between two things. (S, Mgh, Msb, K. *) And A wide tract of land or ground; as also 🡻فَجْوَآءُ: (K:) or a wide and depressed tract thereof; and thus, accord. to Th, the word means in the Kur xviii. 16. (TA.) And The court, or yard, of a house. (S, Msb, K.) And The part between the two sides of the solid hoof. (ISd, K, TA.) The pl. is فَجَوَاتٌ (Msb, K, TA) and فِجَآءٌ. (K, TA. [To these pls. the CK strangely adds, as another, فَجَأٌ.])
